reviews.review_text: I've slept on this game for too long, and now that I've finally beaten it, I must say this game was worth the hype.

It just wasn’t my type of game.

Not a bad game, and I can understand why so many people love Undertale. Unfortunately, the overall style, gameplay, and pacing just didn’t really click with me personally. I tried to get into it because of how highly praised it is, but after spending time with it, I can't seem to enjoy playing it. It just didn’t connect with me the way it seems to for a lot of other players.

Incredibly overrated. That's not to say I think Undertale is a bad game, I think it's quite good in fact! It just isn't as amazing as people make it out to be, but that's just me.

Also, the fanbase is absolutely godawful, but you knew that already. xP

This game has a good story that kept me surprised. I found the middle of the game a little boring though, but I pushing through was totally worth it. The game has a unique art style and some cool mechanics. It's big for a reason.

UNDERTALE. A cultural phenomenon.

A game with a story that is still praised to this day. 

And to this day, my friends still believe I am a genocide player.
Best game I have played, almost made me tear up at the ending. Only doing Pacifist tho, I dont want to do others
This game has become a favorite for a lot of people, but for me, it is a source of inspiration. When I realize that this game was created by only one person, I am filled with determination
Such a Wonderful experience around when it came out and playing it again gives a severe rush of nostalgia. 10/10 will play again.
I played this on Switch and I loved it lots may play it here in the future. Played it again and loved it more

THE MOST AMAZING GAME EVER,undertale, is PACKED full with secrets and easter
it doesnt seem like it should be good but it IS TRUST ME BRO

A very compelling and deeply emotional story wrapped up in spaghetti code and a bow of determination
Toby Fox is a musical genius, comic, and a great storyteller/worldbuilder.
This game has amazing story, characters, and boss fights. Through Determination, you can complete this game, and all of it's endings. Thanks to Toby Fox for this masterpiece of a game.
Amazing game. Worth it to buy, espicialy if on sale.

I thought i had more time in this game, maybe i pirated it or something?

anyway 🏳️‍⚧️ there was signs lmao, thank you Toblerone fox.

Despite everything, it's still you.
One of the best stories I've had the gracious opportunity to listen and play, it is definitely unique and I would recommend it to anyone who hasn't tried this game!
not much I can say that has not been said already except that undertale is an amazing and beautiful game
the game was great! the story was great, the pacing was great, and the true meaning behind every choice you make teaches such a great life lesson definitely recommend!!

Highly recommended. I would say it's kind of a fighting game but not in the classic sense, as someone who typically doesn't enjoy the fighting/hero games, the storyline and characters make it so enjoyable (especially since there is also Delta Rune now, too.) I like the fact it reminds me of old PSP games (idk why just has that vibe for me) and just in general a fun game to try out wether you like to be combative or passive with your storyline.
different routes, dioluge and fights, and the music is absolutely PEAK would DEFFINITLY RECOMEND and after that try deltarune

W style
W soundtracks
W characters
overall... W game
The music is amazing iconic and whenever hear a certain tune from the series I can remember what I was doing in the game at that point

Undertale is a masterpiece of a game.

The characters are all very likable, incredibly written, and have some amazing dialogue. The boss fights are interesting, and the bullet patterns, while all similar, all manage to stand out in their own unique way. Sparing and fighting are all great mechanics for a game, and, if you have no life, you can use a combination of them to go get all 95 endings. The music is some of the best music I've seen in any videogame, Toby Fox is clearly a composer before a coder. And the story is wonderful, an emotional yet often humerus story that has affected millions around the world. 

If my yapping hasn't convinced you already, go buy and play Undertale!
One of the greatest and unique games of all time, the most charming characters, and very fun gameplay. Couldn't recommend more.

10/10.
Amazing game, Great lore story and funny characters. One of my favorite games ever.

I didn't know you could be so talented to the point were you can make tens of thousands of people cry over a fictional character, but Toby Fox did exactly that. 

An experience (not exp, that would be evil) I cant forget. 100/10.

This game is the representation of determination, it's creator, toby fox, didn't even know how to program, but he had a vision, he had determination to make his wish come true, he made the game to best of his abilities, and he made an incredible game, with incredible story, and even deeper backstory. Would recomend.

You know, every now and then I like to return to Undertale and replay it once again. It's because of how much I enjoyed myself in this game that I avoided Deltarune spoilers like fire and eventually got to play it blind once it came to Steam.
This hillarious indie game made me laugh, made me sad, it made me feel. And well, I hope other people can enjoy it too. Ignore all the weird stuff by other fans and just explore the game how they want.
Also, do tell me your favourite character in the comments. Mine is Papayrus cause he is such a funny and wholesome goober xD

You can also choose different routes in different playthroughs but what sets it apart from other games is that if you kill every monster the game will remember and trying to do a pacifist route alters the ending. The only real problem is that now whenever someone uses the word "Determination" I flinch and also the final boss of the neutral route is kinda scary

Perfect music, fun boss fights, game doesn't get boring, good replayability with genocide and true pacifists runs. Would HIGHLY recommend.

TL;DR: I couldn't get into the game and had to force myself to play it. I'm not saying it's a bad game, but it's just not the game for me.

I’ve wanted to play Undertale for a long time, but unfortunately, it turns out it's just not for me. I know it has a 96% positive rating on Steam (which was one of the reasons I finally bought it), and I’m definitely not saying it’s a bad game. It just didn't suit my taste at this moment.

There are things I actually appreciate about it. I like the idea of combat where you have to look for solutions how to beat your opponent without attacking them and you small bullet-hell mode. I like the soundtrack also, it's really good and pleasant to listen to while playing. The humor in the game is 50/50 for me, sometimes it made me laugh, sometimes it was cringe for me.

So why I didn't finish the game and put a negative review? Honestly, because I had to force myself to play it. I know that sounds pretty vague, but for me, the game just lacks that "it" factor. I couldn't get invested in the world or care enough to explore it. I just didn't get the hype. Right before writing this review, I wanted to give it one more shot, but I realized I just really didn't want to boot it up. I didn't have that urge to jump back in and see what happens next, and that convinced me it was time to uninstall it.

I feel like this game says something about people that no game else does. I come back to this game every now and again because of the characters and how deeply I feel for every single one of them still after who knows how many years. But in the end I go against the message of the game that is to let these characters go. Undertale as a whole is truly one of the most comforting games you could ever afford to happen upon, violence aside. Even if you're coming just to kill Sans.
got to papyrus for now and i can say it's great because of the story and humor, also the music slaps.

"you're really kind of a freak, huh?"

Eu só decidi jogar isso, pois alguém que eu gosto muito até demais, gosta desse jogo, porém, essa pessoa não gosta mais de mim. eu decidi parar de jogar esse jogo por me lembrar dela, depois de muito tempo eu decidi rejogar para matar todos do jogo, para quer pagar de "vilão", porém ao entrar em batalha com a Toriel eu me lembrei dos momentos que eu tive com a pessoa, então decidi jogar o jogo certo, fiquei presos em "fases" nesse jogo porém isso e só porque eu sou ruim, a história é interesse, não é a melhor, porém tem seus pontos, todos os npcs "principais" são carismáticos, em especial Toriel, ela me lembra da pessoa que eu gosto, outro que é muito carismático Mettaton, sua batalha foi a melhor do jogo para mim, eu fiquei apenas uma semana nele, e pra mim o personagem que me simpatizei com ele foi Asgore, ate esse exato momento estou preso nele, toda vez que luto contra ele eu toco uma música que ela escolheu para ser "nossa", eu odiava esse jogo, porém, cada vez mais fui gostando, o melhor desse jogo é as músicas, todas são boas, isso foi mais um desabafo do que um comentário, então sim, esse jogo é bom, não é melhor do mundo, porém é ótimo para criar memórias.

Undertale isn’t just a game — it’s an experience that stays with you long after the credits roll.

At first it looks like a simple retro RPG, but the characters, music, humor, and emotional moments are on another level. Every choice actually matters, and the game constantly surprises you in ways most RPGs never do. The soundtrack is genuinely one of the best I’ve heard in gaming.

What makes Undertale special is how attached you become to the world and its characters. One moment you’re laughing at a joke, and the next you’re questioning every decision you made.

Funny, emotional, creative, and unforgettable.

10/10 — you owe it to yourself to play this at least once.
This game isn't about killing people, but actually about petting dogs. Fym genocide route? You clearly aren't very good at petting dogs

Most iconic pixel game of the 2010s whether you like it or not. Toby Fox will go down in history as one of the most endearing game developers of his generation. It's like a lightning in a bottle and it'll forever amaze me that one man is able to create a game such as this. 

Over glazing aside, you'll like the characters, you'll enjoy the atmosphere, it'll get emotional. It's the full package. Also you'll see why fans love it...a little too much...

I first played this game when I was in middle school. I'm now in my twenties and every year or so I return to this game. I feel so much nostalgia every time I play it, and it never gets old. The game has so much heart, so much creativity, and the soundtrack and design is amazing. Undertale is one of those games you have to play at least once in your life. I can't believe it's been 11 years.

After playing through this game on both the Pacifist and Genocide routes, I finally understand why people like this game and why it's so popular.

I absolutely love this game's writing, to which there's so much that's good about it that I don't know where to start. Each primary character is memorable to some degree, and it's very interesting to read their dialogue to get to understand them more. This is one of the few games where I've actually stopped to read dialogue rather than skip to the gameplay. These characters genuinely have depth that gets you to connect with them.

On top of that, I like the humor present throughout the game, such as in the ruins, where you read a sign that says "Three out of four grey rocks recommend you push them", a few moments after which you push grey rocks onto switches, when suddenly a rock starts talking back to you, reluctant to be pushed, or in Hotland, where alongside a conveyor belt, you can interact with a plant, with the text saying "You stop to smell the flowers". It's little jokes like these that set the overall atmosphere of the game, being lighthearted a good amount of the time, yet not being afraid to be emotionally heavy when it needs to be.

In terms of gameplay, I like the battle system and the diverse ways you spare enemies, such as rolling on the ground to smell like a dog to get two guard dogs to leave you alone, continuously fleeing from Undyne into Hotland until she faints and you give her water, or eating a spider donut to get one of Muffet's spiders to show that you donated to her cause so that she lets you go.

What's also notable about the battle system is, of course, the enemy attacks, which take on a wide array of formats.

Honestly, there's way too much to compliment about this game, so I'll just say this: play this game before you die.
